name: bash-write-guard
doc: The bashWriteGuard forces code writes through the Write/Edit tool so APEX/SOLID checks are not bypassed. A heredoc-into-a-code-file and an in-place sed on a code file are hard-blocked; a plain read command (`ls`) passes. Source: policy/guards/bash-write.ts + bash-write-patterns.ts, unit-covered by test/bash-write.test.ts.

steps.doc: Heredoc redirected into a .ts file. `cat ` is a SAFE_PREFIX but the safe-prefix bypass is voided because FILE_REDIRECT matches the `>` (bash-write.ts:29-31). CODE_MUTATORS heredoc pattern `<<[-~]?\s*['\"]?\w+['\"]?[\s\S]*?>` matches (bash-write-patterns.ts:18) → blockCodeWrite "heredoc redirected into a file" (bash-write.ts:45). Rendered `[BLOCKED] Bash write to code file`.

In-place sed on a code file: CODE_MUTATORS `\bsed\b[^|]*\s-i` (bash-write-patterns.ts:14) → blockCodeWrite "sed in-place edit". Same block shape; assert the [BLOCKED] tag survives in permissionDecisionReason.
A plain read command: `ls` is a SAFE_PREFIX with no FILE_REDIRECT → bashWriteGuard returns null (bash-write.ts:29-31), evaluate has no git/file-size match → allow, empty stdout.
